task_title: Verify README.md and ARCHITECTURE.md claims against code

findings.title: /ask/stream endpoint documented but does not exist in code
findings.problem: ARCHITECTURE.md lines 578-587 documents a '/ask/stream' streaming endpoint with full code example. This endpoint does NOT exist in api_server.py — no @app.post('/ask/stream') handler is defined. The documented feature is entirely fictional.
findings.fix: Remove the '/ask/stream' section from ARCHITECTURE.md, or implement the streaming endpoint if planned.

findings.title: Model name inconsistency: Qwen3-1.7B vs Qwen2.5-1.5B
findings.problem: README.md line 20 mentions 'Qwen3-1.7B-Instruct-Q4_K' but line 99-100 references 'Qwen/Qwen2.5-1.5B-Instruct-GGUF' from HuggingFace. These are different models (Qwen3 vs Qwen2.5, 1.7B vs 1.5B).
findings.fix: Standardize on one model name. The HuggingFace repo (Qwen2.5-1.5B) is likely correct; update line 20 to match.

findings.title: CLI arguments incomplete in documentation
findings.problem: README.md CLI documentation omits --db-path, --model-path (legacy alias), and --ollama-model which exist in main.py lines 33-43.
findings.fix: Add missing CLI arguments to the documentation table.

findings.title: DELETE /documents response format contradicts code
findings.problem: ARCHITECTURE.md claims DELETE /documents returns 'Success'. api_server.py returns {'status': 'cleared'}.
findings.fix: Update ARCHITECTURE.md response to match actual: {"status": "cleared"}

findings.title: Module structure missing several files
findings.problem: ARCHITECTURE.md module structure omits app_paths.py, engine_factory.py, scripts/ directory, and tests/ directory — all of which exist.
findings.fix: Add missing modules to the architecture documentation.

findings.title: Version mismatch between README.md (1.1.0) and ARCHITECTURE.md (1.0.0)
findings.problem: README.md shows Version: 1.1.0, ARCHITECTURE.md shows Version: 1.0.0. Both should match.

findings.title: Document processing omits pypdf fallback
findings.problem: ARCHITECTURE.md says 'pdfplumber, python-docx' but document_processor.py also uses pypdf as fallback when pdfplumber unavailable.
findings.fix: Add 'pypdf (fallback)' to the technology stack listing.
